title: Public Emergencies
chapterTitle: Bill of Rights, Freedoms and Responsibilities

content.text: The Governor may, by proclamation which shall be published in the Gazette, declare that a state of public emergency exists for the purposes of this Part whenever he or she is satisfied that a public emergency has arisen as a result of the occurrence of any earthquake, hurricane, flood, fire, outbreak of pestilence, outbreak of infectious disease or other calamity whether similar to the foregoing or not, or that action has been taken or is immediately threatened by any person or body of persons of such a nature and on so extensive a scale as to be likely to endanger the public safety or to deprive the community, or any substantial portion of the community, of supplies or services essential to life.

notes: Governor power to declare state of emergency.
